ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: O fun ŋɔ, “N daŋni mu sɛm ma Galili pulum bar digi naa Zɛbulun awa Naftalai tima koora dɔ wɛwa. O nyɛn Jɔɔdan jibaŋ kinkin ti tɛɛnɛ nɛɛ a la bɛɛŋ zen ti wati. Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nidamata nyɛn dooni.
translated: The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; those who dwelt in a land of deep darkness, on them has light shone. You have multiplied the nation; you have increased its joy; they rejoice before you as with joy at the harvest, as they are glad when they divide the spoil. For the yoke of his burden, the rod of his shoulder, the staff of his back, you have broken on the day of Midian. For every boot of the booted warrior, every garment rolled in blood, will be burned as fuel for the fire. For to us a child is born, to us a son is given; and the government shall be upon his shoulder, and his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Alizin tagni ta fun a sumi Yesu a ŋɔ, “I duŋni ya a ta, nyɛ ya la zu kurchusi ta ni.”
translated: The woman was still talking to her friend when she saw Jesus and said, “Look, I have been telling you about him.”

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: O kunyan ti fun na paan a du un ni a ŋɔ, ‘I waa ya nibin tagin awa zanyɛrka tiinnɛ. I lisi di mu kum kina doonani mu vɛ duuli a kpa be naa mu vɛ tugi.
translated: The one who gives you a thousand dollars says, ‘I want you to use this money to buy me a bicycle with a bell and a very good tire. I want you to use the remaining money to buy a goat and a rope.’

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: O fun a chaasi kin naa maga nɛnɛ wa si sɛm ni a biksi ba. Pita fun yir un a li cheŋcheŋa a galimi un.

text: O fu kpa tulale nɔ a yogli malɛ damata a fu kpa a a nyɛ fara tim ni, chen fu wa bɔɔ?”
translated: What will you do if you see your mother being beaten by someone, will you accept it?

text: a kpa sak pɛŋ ni a wa nyɛ nihabichɛl ti ni. O fun kpa la nyɛ o niin ti ni.
translated: My father gave me a bag of beans. He also gave me a calabash.

text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni a na chenani funɛ ya alizin tagni ta awa kurchusi ta, ba fun ŋɔ ba naa wa chen.
translated: When you see a person who does not have a good character, do not imitate him, because his ways are not good.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ma ya womniwa a chi ba naa ya nyuluma. Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam naa o nyɛn wa kaali salma malɛ naa nyɛn dooni chen.
translated: I am happy to see you. My friend came and told me that you are going to come, and I have prepared a little food for you to eat when you arrive.

text: Chen saha ti nidamata se wa zi bu wa su min. Ba zi wa li dɔŋ gantɛlau a ha dɔŋa.
translated: The sun was setting behind the mountain and the people were going home. They were tired and hungry.

text: Chiksɛ nɔ kpok kaali a maga.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “O funɛ vusiu ka tumtumsi ta kunyan bur wa a fun yir ba ba lagmi o wati. O fun pɛɛsi ba chenani ba kpa o malɛ ta a tum.
translated: The person who has a bad heart will not be able to do good to anyone, not even to their own family. A person with a bad heart will only bring destruction to themselves.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.

translated: The man gave John a bag of beans to take to his mother, but on the way, a rat ran out with it.

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “Nara maga nu mu sɛm na aa tiŋ a, ba waa chi nibin nɛɛgi naa funɛ sa o dama a zɔŋ gbɛntɛl nyuhu ni chen.
translated: My mother said that if I don't behave, she will give me to the chief so that he will beat me and I will know her.

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Min ya un. Nyɛ mu ŋɔ hin di wuzu digi i zi wa na mu waa nibine beu ka mu kani Koosa nihɛɛ bar kani ni, a bukoo na min n wa bur wa dobaala tuhu ni.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “I am the one. If you are looking for me, let these people go. This is to fulfill the words he had spoken: “Not one of those you gave me have I lost.”

text: a sumi un kinkan a ŋɔ, “Mu nihabichɛla la wi a daŋni o sou. Mu sumi hinu kinkan, kuwa a wa kpa i nisa a sak un ni, chen ni o zi na alaafɛu.”
translated: “My mother once said to me, “When I was still small, my mother used to pound a certain grain, and she would give me some to eat, and I would chew it with my friends, and then we would go and play outside.”

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Nihaan ti fun ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, o ya wutirɛ, amaa vasi ta ka yaa di diwe naa bichɛsi ta yaa di ka a chesi chen.”
translated: My mother said to me, “My child, you are going, you must take a bag with you and if you get hungry on the way you will eat from it.”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ un, “O bɔu.” Pita funɛ zu gbaari Yesu ni dam ti ni di o ŋɔ un chenani yɛɛ, Yesu fun te pɛɛsi un, “Simɔn Pita, anti bɔ lampo a nyɛ pulum nɔ nihɛɛ ti ni? Hɔɔra koo pulum ti timaa? Yɛrɛ i lisi?”
translated: Peter said to him, “Lord.” Peter’s companion, the one whom Jesus loved, was reclining next to him. Jesus said to him, “Simon Peter, do you love me more than these? Tend my lambs. Are you still a slave to worldly things? What are you waiting for? Follow me.”

text: Adaha koor ti fun yir o tumtum ti di o wa o wati. O fun wa, koor ti ŋɔ un, ‘I ya tumtum yɔrirɛ. I funɛ sumi min chene, n fun gil malɛ naa maga i fun pɛŋ n wati a nyɛ hin.

text: Yesu fun burgi a nyini a go o gantɛl tiŋsi ta a ŋɔ Pita ni, “Tur dooni, domi i lisi chi sintannɛ. Chenani i sɛm chen wa li Koosa wati. A li nibinsa watɛɛ.”
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into his harvest field."

text: “Ma anti la zi ya wuti tiina awa ninɛɛgɛ a li kin nama maga ni? O chi dam tiina naa lisɛ o tumtumsa nidigi di o nyini o dam nara a kinyɛ ba diwe ba diwe diili saha.
translated: “What is the meaning of this that I am seeing, is it a sign or what? This thing I am seeing is confusing me, my mind is not clear, I don’t understand what it means, it keeps on changing, changing, what is it?”

text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn dooni a lisi ba tusa ni, “O sɛm sɛma nan ni, o kpa o titirɛ a chiŋsi Koosa bar chiŋi ni.”
translated: The dog's mother and father were very happy and said to their children, “You should go and look for food at Koosa's house.”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yesu fun burgi a pɛɛsi ba, “Nar kpa haan ka o chɛnsa tusa bu chɔkuu? Aai. Ba wa zi ya chen. Saha naa ba zi wa kpak baal ti a kaali, chen wuzu ba zi wa vɔu nɔnau.”
translated: “Why did you doubt me, Thomas? You have seen me and still you doubt. Blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe. They will be given many blessings and a special place in heaven.”

translated: The man said, “You are the one I have been looking for. If you are a person who has a strong heart, then let us go and see who will be the one to kill the other. I will show you my power and my strength.”
